text.title: Research Repo Naming — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules every research-repo filename must satisfy. The rules exist to make artefacts retrievable by both grep and RAG agents without opening the file.

rule.statement: Filenames use only lowercase ascii letters, digits, and hyphens. No spaces, no Cyrillic, no apostrophes, no parentheses, no underscores at the boundary segments.

rationale.source: cross-platform filesystem compatibility; OpenAI/Anthropic file-search practice

rule.rationale: Mixed-case and special characters break tooling on macOS-vs-Linux casefolding, fail in URL-based stores (S3, Drive sharing), and confuse regex-based RAG ingestion. The slug rule is a pure win.

rule.statement: Every file starts with ISO date `YYYY-MM-DD-` referring to the study-event date (interview date, survey close, observation date), NOT the file-creation date.

rationale.source: ResearchOps Community pattern; archival best practice

rule.rationale: Sort order = chronological by event. File-creation date drifts when files are exported, copied, or re-edited. Study-event date is the only stable anchor and the one synthesis cares about.

rule.statement: Filename participant identifier MUST be of the form `P###` (zero-padded) or `P-<hash-8>`. Real names, emails, employer names, or phone numbers in filenames are forbidden.

rationale.source: GDPR Art. 5(1)(c); IRB practice; common sense

rule.rationale: Filenames are listed in directory previews, shared in screen captures, and indexed by every backup tool. Real names in filenames are a routine PII leak path. The participant identifier is a foreign key to a separately-stored map.

rule.statement: Filename includes a study-type segment from the closed enum: `int` (interview), `usab` (usability), `surv` (survey), `diary`, `intercept`, `analytics`, `synth` (synthesis output), `cs` (case-study).

rationale.source: Dovetail tagging practice; Atomic Research taxonomy

rule.rationale: Closed enums beat free-form because they survive the year. A researcher tagged their own files as "user-talk" / "user-chat" / "convo" in three weeks — the synthesis agent could not group them. The enum is small enough to memorise.

rule.statement: For multi-language repos, filename ends with `.<lang>.<ext>` where `<lang>` is the 2-letter ISO code (`uk`, `en`, `pl`, `pt`). Monolingual repos may omit; mixed repos MUST include.

rationale.source: i18n filename practice; LLM cross-lingual retrieval research (XLM-R, mBERT)

rule.rationale: Cross-lingual RAG retrieves better when language is explicit in metadata; LLM judges score quotes correctly when the language is known. Mixed-language transcripts without language tag get translated when nobody asked.

section.title: Filename grammar
section.code:
filename ::= date "-" project "-" type "-" participant ("-" descriptor)? ("." lang)? "." ext date ::= YYYY "-" MM "-" DD project ::= [a-z0-9]+ ("-" [a-z0-9]+)* -- e.g. nero, faion-cli, podcast type ::= "int" | "usab" | "surv" | "diary" | "intercept" | "analytics" | "synth" | "cs" participant ::= ("p" digit{3}) | ("p-" hex{8}) | "na" -- "na" for non-participant artefacts descriptor ::= [a-z0-9]+ ("-" [a-z0-9]+)* -- short slug, optional lang ::= "uk" | "en" | "pl" | "pt" | ... ext ::= "md" | "txt" | "json" | "mp3" | "mp4" | "vtt" | "csv" | "pdf"
example: 2026-05-12-nero-int-p012-onboarding-pain.uk.md example: 2026-05-14-podcast-synth-na-3-jobs-clusters.en.md

example.title: Three artefacts, one study
example.code: 2026-05-12-nero-int-p012-raw.uk.vtt -- raw transcript 2026-05-12-nero-int-p012-tagged.uk.md -- tagged by researcher 2026-05-12-nero-int-p012-quotes.en.md -- quote pack translated for synthesis
example.note: All three rules: ISO date, participant P012, study type int, language explicit. Grep `p012` → all three; grep `int` → all interviews.

example.title: Old-style chaos
example.code: Maria interview 12 May.docx Copy of Maria interview FINAL v3.docx Interview May 2026 Maria notes (1).pdf maria_chat_recording.mp4
example.note: Real name in filename (r3 violation), no ISO date (r2), spaces and parentheses (r1), no study-type (r4), no language (r5). Cannot be RAG-indexed sanely.
